public_note.title: What the G900 6/9 anomaly means
public_note.summary: The G900 phase scan found one clean exception. The pair 6/9 appears as a full-60 phase transition, meaning it occurs across all 60 roots of the relevant slot-12 region. But it is not represented by an admitted Layer 2 boundary carrier, and in the tested carrier-rail language it has no internal delta-edge support. This makes 6/9 a real phase-grammar event, not a channel.
body: The G900 Observatory now separates three things that are easy to confuse.
First, there is phase grammar. A phase transition is real when it appears reproducibly in the edge-limit scan. On that test, 6/9 is real.
Second, there is carrier support. A transition is supported when its step is witnessed by the tested internal or boundary carrier rails. On that test, nine of the ten full-60 packages are supported.
Third, there is channel admission. A channel would require explicit registry admission. No channel has been admitted.

The 6/9 package is the exception. It appears in the phase grammar with full 60-root support, but it is absent from the Layer 2 boundary-carrier registry and has no tested internal delta-edge support. It is therefore best read as an unsupported internal phase step inside the slot-12 composite triangle.
In plain terms: the motion is visible, but the current carrier language does not explain it.
That is not a failure. It is a useful boundary. The Observatory can now say exactly where the current carrier grammar works, and exactly where it stops.
boundary: This note does not admit a channel.
This note does not mutate the G900 body.
This note does not claim a force.
This note does not make a physics claim.
This note records a bounded grammar exception with a reproducible audit trail.
public_note.short_copy: The 6/9 anomaly is real in the G900 phase grammar, but unsupported by the tested carrier rails. It is not a channel. It marks the exact place where the current Layer 2 carrier language stops explaining the observed phase motion.
public_note.keeper_statement: The 6/9 package is a full-60 phase transition package that is real in the phase grammar, absent from the Layer 2 boundary-carrier registry, and fully unsupported by tested internal delta-edge support. It is not a channel.
